what_it_does: Records six independently convergent design decisions for an annotation/commenting substrate, framed as a noticeboard item under MAP1, and notes which analytical lenses reached each conclusion.
observations: Front matter declares id item-15f21a74, type block, source claude_code, state current, channel dragnet-development, and a part_of link to board://item-1deff1b2.
Title 'MAP1 · Strong convergences (independent agreement = signal)' frames the item as a convergence map.
Body lists six numbered convergence points, each citing multiple independent 'lenses' and sometimes specific systems (Figma, Docs, GitHub, W3C, iOS).
Point 1 (anchor by quote + content-hash, re-resolved at read time) is labeled the single most-agreed technical decision.
Point 3 asserts the document tree does not exist yet and that the brief's claim 'the model already exists' is false for multi-scale selection.
Point 4 rejects long-press-as-primary in favor of tap-for-default with long-press to escalate.
Point 5 calls for a durable draft-queue, submit-all/send-now, and a visible reply inbox.
Point 6 proposes thread/resolution state as a fold of the observation thread using the mark-is-truth pattern.
No code symbols are present; the file is a design memo only.
standouts: Quote-plus-content-hash anchoring re-resolved at read time is the most cross-lens agreed technical decision.
Intent-typed envelopes (comment vs change-request with proposed diff) are treated as the mechanism that closes the 'update this specific thing' gap.
The flat one-level block-list surface is asserted to contradict the brief's assumption of an existing document tree.
Tap-default / long-press-escalate is the preferred gesture model.
A durable draft queue plus visible reply inbox is the one feature the minimalist lens considers worth adding now.
Thread/resolution state is modeled as a fold over the existing observation thread.
conventions: Follows the repository's YAML-frontmatter noticeboard format with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history fields.
Uses board:// URI addressing and a part_of link to a parent item.
Uses markdown body with a numbered list and lens citations.
Breaks from pure code artifacts by containing no code symbols and relying on informal 'lens' references.
concerns: Strong claims (e.g., 'the brief is false') are asserted without inline evidence or links to the code/lens outputs that verified them.
The 'lenses' are named but their full arguments are not included, so the independence of agreement cannot be checked from this file alone.
Created and updated timestamps are identical and history has only the 'filed' event, giving no revision context.
No code symbols or concrete examples are embedded, so the file is a high-level design assertion rather than an auditable artifact.
notes: A design-convergence memo that turns independent cross-lens agreement into six candidate requirements for an annotation system, while flagging that the existing document model may be less complete than the brief assumes.
questions: What are the exact definitions and outputs of the 'lenses' cited (annotation-tools, skeptic, graph-architect, pro-annotator, iOS, voice, minimalist)?
Which codebase or surface was verified to confirm the flat one-level block list?
What is the 'brief' and where is its claim that 'the model already exists' stated?
What is the parent item board://item-1deff1b2 and how does this item relate to it?
What does 'mark-is-truth pattern' refer to in this context?
How was 'independent agreement' counted and how was overlap distinguished from convergence?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—collect and present strong convergences where independent lenses land in the same place—matches the numbered list of agreed design decisions and the signal framing.
apparent_themes: Independent agreement as a proxy for high-confidence design signal
Robust anchoring by content rather than stored position
Explicit intent typing for annotation envelopes
Gesture design: tap default, long-press escalate
Closed-loop feedback: draft queue, batch submit, reply inbox
Thread/resolution state folded from observation thread
intention_signals: To consolidate multiple analytical perspectives into a concise set of high-confidence design constraints for an annotation/commenting system, and to expose a potentially false premise in the project brief so it can be addressed before building on it.
novelty: The use of 'lenses that never spoke' as an epistemic device, the explicit ranking of convergence by number of independent lenses, and the direct contradiction of a brief assumption about an existing document tree.
contribution: A structured set of candidate design decisions for an annotation substrate, each annotated with the lenses that converged on it, plus a meta-method of using independent cross-lens agreement to surface decisions worth keeping in a fused design.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item titled MAP1 that enumerates six strong cross-lens convergences for an annotation/commenting system, including quote-hash anchoring, intent-typed envelopes, a flat document surface, tap-default gestures, a closed-loop draft/reply loop, and folded thread resolution state.
intention_for_embedding: To capture high-confidence design decisions reached independently by multiple analytical lenses and to flag a disputed assumption in the project brief.
